sections.title: Types of Charts and Graphs
items: Bar Graph: Rectangles represent a variable; height/length is proportional to value. X-axis = value; Y-axis = categories. Bars are independent of each other.
Pictogram: Uses images instead of bars. The legend tells you the value each image represents. Half of an image = half the value.
Histogram: Presents values in ranges (not exact values). Bars have no gaps between them. X-axis = range of values; Y-axis = frequency.
Pie Graph: Represents how a whole value is divided into portions. Percentages are placed in each portion. To find the amount: multiply the total by the percentage.
Line Graph: Represents a trend or constant change over time. The line shows direction (increasing, decreasing, fluctuating).
Table: Direct presentation of data. Best used when data is simple or contains non-quantifiable information (names, descriptions, types).
